design_principles: System composition turns the wired object graph into a running application
create_app() is the sole factory — it is the composition boundary between wiring and runtime
build_container() is called exactly once inside create_app() — use case instances are shared across all requests
Route handlers call use cases exclusively — no domain objects, repositories, or adapters are imported in the API layer
Exception handlers are registered at app level — route handlers never catch use-case exceptions themselves
The API layer contains no domain logic — request validation is structural (Pydantic), semantic validation belongs to use cases and domain
ASGI server (uvicorn) is the runtime host — the entry point is main.py at the workspace root
System composition must not reinvent what dependency wiring already assembled — it only bootstraps and starts

runtime_type.mode: synchronous — no background tasks, no async queues

bootstrap_sequence.detail: uvicorn imports main.py and resolves the `app` module-level variable
main.py calls create_app() which creates the FastAPI instance
create_app() calls build_container() — assembles full object graph (all infrastructure, adapters, use cases). See dependency_wiring.json for the full wiring order.
build_router(container) creates an APIRouter with all 3 route handlers bound to the wired use cases. The router is included in the app with app.include_router().
All 9 domain exception handlers are registered on the FastAPI app instance. They map use-case exceptions to HTTP status codes and structured error responses.
create_app() returns the fully configured FastAPI instance. uvicorn begins serving requests.

entry_point.content: import sys, os; sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'src')); from shell.api.app import create_app; app = create_app()
entry_point.note: src/ is inserted into sys.path so all bounded-context packages resolve without installation. The `app` variable is the ASGI application instance consumed by uvicorn.

route_handlers.description: Creates and fully initialises a ConversationSession. Loads user, school, history. Pre-seeds DecisionContext in BC-2. Returns session_id for all subsequent calls.

Submits a user message. Runs the full RAG turn pipeline: intent classification → decision intelligence → knowledge retrieval → response generation → evaluation. Returns the grounded assistant response.
Transitions the session to the closed terminal state. Only idle sessions can be closed. The requesting user must own the session.

fields.name: groundedness_score
fields.note: 0.0–1.0; how well the response is grounded in retrieved evidence

fields.name: relevance_score
fields.note: 0.0–1.0; how well the response answers the user's question

exception_handler_registry.registration_rule: Registered at app level — route handlers never catch exceptions

handlers.note: Aliased as TurnSessionNotFoundError in app.py to avoid name collision with EH-5
Aliased as CloseSessionNotFoundError in app.py to avoid name collision with EH-4. Same HTTP mapping.

request_flow.description: Every HTTP request follows the same strict inward-only path through the architecture layers.
flow: 2. FastAPI routes to the matching handler in router.py
3. Pydantic validates the request body schema (structural validation only)
4. Route handler builds a use-case Command from request fields
6. Use case orchestrates domain objects, adapters, and repositories
7. Use case returns a Response dataclass
8. Route handler maps Response fields to a Pydantic response schema
9. FastAPI serializes the response to JSON and returns it
10. If a domain exception is raised at step 6, the registered exception handler intercepts it and returns a structured ErrorResponse
request_flow.dependency_direction: HTTP → router.py → use_case → domain / infrastructure (never upward)

in_scope: Nine exception-to-HTTP-status mappings registered at app level
ASGI entry point in main.py at workspace root
out_of_scope: Authentication / authorization middleware — user_id is treated as pre-resolved trusted identity at prototype scope
Rate limiting — not required at prototype scope
CORS configuration — not required unless a browser client is added
Health check endpoint — not required by the deliverable
GraphQL, CLI, or webhook entry points — REST is the sole delivery channel
Background tasks or async queues — the RAG pipeline is synchronous by design

pending_from_dependency_wiring.summary: SQLiteRAGMetricsLogger (ADAPT-15) is implemented but not wired into ProcessTurnUseCase or build_container(). The system composition layer itself needs no change for this gap — it is resolved entirely within the dependency wiring (container.py) and the ProcessTurnUseCase constructor. System composition is not affected.

must: build_container() called once inside create_app() — never at module level or per-request
All exception handlers registered inside create_app() before the app is returned
Route handlers receive the container by constructor injection via build_router(container)
API layer imports only: composition container, use-case command/response classes, Pydantic schemas
must_not: No domain logic in route handlers or app factory
No repository, adapter, or infrastructure import in src/shell/api/
No per-request wiring — object graph is immutable after startup
No try/except blocks in route handlers — exceptions bubble to app-level handlers
